abstract: Colonies of the red harvester ant regulate foraging activity based on food availability and local conditions. Here we quantified tissue content of 4 biogenic amines (dopamine, serotonin, octopamine, and tyramine) in single forager brains from 9 red harvester ant colonies collected in the field. Capillary electrophoresis coupled with fast-scan cyclic voltammetry (CE-FSCV) was used to separate and detect the amines. Overall, dopamine content was highest, followed by octopamine, serotonin, and tyra

methods.name: Field observation and behavioral assays
methods.description: Applied field observation and behavioral assays approach
